title: survival_run.bat does not redirect stdout — log file is stale/empty
why: survival_run.bat:19 launches `start ... python -u run_survival_live.py ...` with NO stdout/stderr redirection, yet line 22 tells the operator 'Live log: logs\survival_service.log'. That file is never written via the .bat path (output goes to the minimized window). Confirms the MEMORY note 'survival log file is stale'. survival_service.ps1 redirects correctly; the two launchers diverge and the .bat is the one described as the manual double-click launcher.
value_rationale: Operator-facing: under the freeze the .bat is a plausible launch path and its own banner lies about where the log is. Low blast radius (state files are the source of truth) but actively misleading during an incident. Medium.
scope: Redirect stdout+stderr in survival_run.bat to logs\survival_service.log (and .err), matching survival_service.ps1.
Or remove the false 'Live log' banner line if redirection is intentionally omitted.
Keep -u (unbuffered) so the redirected log is live.
recommendation: Add `>> logs\survival_service.log 2>&1` (or the `start` equivalent) mirroring the .ps1.

history.note: From hackathon code audit, finding A2

history.reason: fix(hackathon): B0177 â€” run_loop resilience + lottery envelope latch tightening
survival_run.bat now wraps python in `cmd /c` redirecting stdout+stderr (merged, incl [ALARM]) to logs/survival_service.log; banner corrected.
fix(hackathon): B0178/B0179/B0180 â€” launcher logging, ps1 Stage-2 passthrough, crypto_var_cap drift
